channel.title: Latest alerts from washingtonpost.com
channel.description: An unofficial feed created by Derek Willis.

item.title: Can I cut the mold off cheese and eat the rest? Ask us your cooking questions.
item.description: Every Thursday at 11 a.m. Eastern, Becky Krystal answers your cooking questions.

item.title: An ICE detainee sought treatment for mental illness. He died in a struggle with guards.
item.description: The family of Geraldo Lunas Campos is suing detention center operators and guards over his death. Records show he repeatedly requested treatment for his mental illness.

item.title: 4 proven health benefits of drinking coffee
item.description: Hundreds of studies have shown that a cup of joe — or more! — every day may protect against some major diseases.

item.title: With 76,475 dead, Canada appears to find its line on euthanasia
item.description: A parliamentary committee recommends against expanding it to psychiatric patients.

item.title: This song is the star of today’s On the Record
item.description: This song is the star of today’s On the Record

item.title: The labor market grew less than expected in June
item.description: The U.S. economy added 57,000 jobs last month as employers struggled to keep up momentum in the labor market. The unemployment rate ticked down to 4.2 percent, according to Labor Department data released Thursday.

item.title: Trump’s wealth. Kyiv strikes. Detainee death. July Fourth. World Cup. Coffee benefits. Taylor and Travis. Catch up now.
item.description: The 7.

item.title: U.S. advances in World Cup with 2-0 victory over Bosnia, despite playing a man down for much of second half
item.description: Folarin Balogun, the team’s dynamic striker, scored the first goal of the match, but picked up a red card in the 62nd minute. The Americans will next face Belgium in a round-of-16 showdown Monday, but they will be without Balogun, who will have to sit out the U.S. men’s team’s most anticipated World Cup match in a quarter-century.

item.title: July Fourth fireworks on the Mall are likely to cause hazardous air pollution, documents show
item.description: Internal National Park Service modeling predicts dangerous pollution around the Mall and “very unhealthy” conditions across central D.C.

item.title: I love my husband, but my marriage is costing me
item.description: I love my husband, but my marriage is costing me

item.title: They built the world’s most powerful AI. They’re facing a mystery they can’t explain.
item.description: Anthropic, Google and Meta have hired computer scientists, neuroscientists and philosophers to study what some in the industry think may become a moral crisis.

item.title: Former CIA director John Brennan sues Justice Department over investigation targeting him
item.description: Brennan’s attorneys asked a judge to order top officials at the Justice Department to preserve all records related to the investigation of the Obama-era intelligence director. They said they intend to use those files as part of a defense argument that any prosecution of Brennan stems from a vindictive drive by President Donald Trump’s administration to seek revenge against a longtime critic.

item.title: See which 7 cities could soon see record-hot days and nights
item.description: Parts of the Midwest had Dubai-like humidity on Tuesday. Here’s where that’s headed next.

item.title: Every food stall is a mystery at the Great American State Fair, our critics write
item.description: We set out to find the best, worst and most bizarre bites at a state fair that is unlike any other. Confusing signage didn't help.

item.title: ICE’s arrest of nun heading to church fuels bipartisan backlash
item.description: Sister Leticia “Letty” Ugboaja was released hours after being detained in South Texas. The president’s deportation drive has riled border areas that supported him in 2024, and a GOP congresswoman joined Democrats calling for Ugboaja’s release.

item.title: A 264-year-old George Washington mystery solved
item.description: A military trail transforms into a highway of American progress.

item.title: A majority opinion in today’s On the Record
item.description: A majority opinion in today’s On the Record

item.title: Voters are angry with Washington, and other takeaways from the Colorado primaries
item.description: A sitting Democratic House member and an incumbent senator both lost key races, adding to the angst within the party’s establishment.

item.title: Supreme Court. Primary results. Trump’s income. Heat dome. World Cup. Healthy diet. Ocean of stars. Catch up now.
item.description: The 7.

item.title: Democratic socialist Melat Kiros upsets 15-term Rep. Diana DeGette in Colorado, adding to recent primary wins for the left
item.description: With a veteran incumbent and a young political newcomer, the House primary highlighted generational divides and drew increased attention after progressive challengers defeated two House Democrats last week in New York.

item.title: Colorado Democrats choose state AG Phil Weiser over Sen. Michael Bennet in governor primary that showed party’s anti-D.C. tilt
item.description: The primary race was seen as a test of whether voters saw Bennet’s experience in Washington as a liability and which of the two Democratic candidates would more aggressively fight President Donald Trump.

item.title: Trump reports over $1.4 billion from cryptocurrency ventures in 2025, a major rise in income since reentering the White House
item.description: The president’s income from crypto, digital tokens and related partnerships last year was reported in financial disclosure forms released by the White House. In his 2024 financial disclosure filed a year ago, Trump reported earning more than $600 million in income.

item.title: The birthright citizenship ruling isn’t a catastrophe
item.description: The government has plenty of mechanisms to discourage birth tourism and illegal immigration.

item.title: Is your ‘stress mindset’ worsening your anxiety? Here’s how to fix it.
item.description: Stress — and how we think about it — may actually help us thrive.

item.title: Judge strikes down Trump’s restrictions on loan forgiveness for public servants
item.description: U.S. District Judge Myong J. Joun sided with a group of nonprofits, states, cities and unions that challenged the legality of the administration’s revised eligibility requirements for Public Service Loan Forgiveness, which cancels the education debt of government and nonprofit employees after 10 years of service and 120 monthly loan payments.

item.title: 5 takeaways from Supreme Court rulings on presidential power, elections and LGBTQ+ rights
item.description: This term, the Supreme Court justices gave President Donald Trump more power — but not as much as he wanted.

item.title: A giant telescope begins a decade-long search for dark matter — and finds an ‘ocean of stars’
item.description: An observatory in Chile is taking a timelapse of the sky over the Southern Hemisphere to help scientists measure the properties of a mysterious cosmic force.

item.title: See the forecast for what could be some of D.C.’s hottest days on record
item.description: The Washington area will swelter this week as the heat dome intensifies.

item.title: Carolyn Hax: Wife’s packed calendar leaves no room for an intimate marriage
item.description: Letter writer’s own connections outside the marriage are limited — a possible cause or effect of the marital drift.

item.title: The deep state didn’t take down Nixon. But why did Vance say it did?
item.description: Courts, Congress and the press drove Watergate. And such a scandal could happen again.

item.title: Supreme Court rules political parties can coordinate spending with candidates, further loosening campaign finance limits
item.description: The justices sided with congressional Republicans in a decision likely to upend how political parties funnel millions of dollars into TV ads in the upcoming midterm elections.

item.title: Supreme Court rejects Trump’s push to overturn birthright citizenship, the principle that almost all born on U.S. soil are American
item.description: President Donald Trump’s executive order would have had sweeping political, economic and social ramifications, changing the definition of citizenship in the most significant way since the 14th Amendment guaranteeing citizenship to the formerly enslaved was ratified shortly after the Civil War.

item.title: Supreme Court upholds bans on transgender women in female athletics
item.description: The ruling in favor of the Idaho and West Virginia laws is the latest blow to the LGBTQ+ community, which has faced repeated losses at the Supreme Court in recent years.

item.title: The Supreme Court is about to issue rulings on birthright citizenship, transgender athletes and more. Follow our coverage.
item.description: The justices will announce a decision in one of the biggest cases of the term, on President Donald Trump’s controversial executive order that said citizenship would not be granted to children born to parents who are in the country illegally or those on temporary visas for work, travel, school or humanitarian reasons.

item.title: Trump is using a $500M no-bid contract to build his ballroom
item.description: The secret agreement was routed through a White House office that typically handles repairs and furnishings and is exempt from competitive bidding requirements.

item.title: Supreme Court. Ballroom contract. Iran talks. Earthquake damage. Wildfires. Stress tips. World Cup. Catch up now.
item.description: The 7.

item.title: What a gastroenterologist wants you to know about bidets
item.description: Columnist Trisha Pasricha has received many questions from people who are bidet-curious but not yet bidet-ready.

item.title: Trump wanted to break the system. The system is breaking him instead.
item.description: The courts, Congress and the Constitution are doing what a weak opposition could not, Ramesh Ponnuru writes.

item.title: We visited the Trump-backed ‘Freedom Trucks’ crisscrossing the country. Here’s what we found.
item.description: At a time of fierce debate over how to teach American history, particularly around issues of race, the Freedom Trucks weigh in squarely on one side of the argument, telling a patriotic, positive story of core American values and exceptionalism. They stand in sharp contrast to liberal efforts in recent years to lift up discussion of systemic racism and highlight chapters where the United States has failed to live up to its ideals.

item.title: See where a heat dome may bring record-breaking temperatures this week
item.description: A dangerous heat dome is expected to intensify over the Eastern United States this week, pushing temperatures into the triple digits and probably breaking hundreds of records across three dozen states. The risk for heat-related impacts is forecast to reach major or extreme levels for more than 220 million Americans in total through Saturday, according to the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration.

item.title: Electric vehicle sales have collapsed, but hybrid vehicles are quietly taking over
item.description: While electric vehicles have been hit hard by shifting policies and drivers’ doubts, sales of hybrids are up 80 percent since 2023. Carmakers are taking notice.

item.title: Carolyn Hax: Stepmom refuses to find more hobbies to foster a healthy retired life
item.description: Carolyn Hax: Stepmom refuses to find more hobbies to foster a healthy retired life

item.title: Medicare is about to cover GLP-1 drugs for weight loss. Here’s what to know.
item.description: Beginning Wednesday, the federal health insurance program will cover GLP-1 drugs for weight loss for the first time, with patients responsible for a $50-a-month co-payment that makes it far cheaper than cash prices without insurance. But there are important considerations under the Trump administration’s 18-month pilot.

item.title: Pete Hegseth’s military purges claim a superstar
item.description: Gen. Christopher Donahue heads into early retirement as the defense secretary seeks to root out MAGA disloyalty.

item.title: Referencing a president in today’s On the Record
item.description: Referencing a president in today’s On the Record

item.title: Supreme Court expands Trump’s power over the federal bureaucracy
item.description: The justices allowed the president to fire a Democratic member of the Federal Trade Commission, striking down a nearly century-old precedent that insulated independent agencies from political influence by the executive.

item.title: Supreme Court, for now, blocks Trump from firing Fed board member Lisa Cook
item.description: The decision is a setback for the president, who has pushed aggressively to exert greater control over parts of the federal government that Congress set up to be insulated from influence by the president. He tried to oust Federal Reserve governor Lisa Cook, alleging she committed mortgage fraud.

item.title: Supreme Court rules mail-in ballots postmarked by Election Day that arrive later can be tallied
item.description: A decision against such ballots could have upended election procedures in states across the country ahead of the midterm elections.

item.title: Supreme Court lets stand $5 million civil verdict against Trump in E. Jean Carroll case
item.description: The high-profile 2023 trial found President Donald Trump liable for allegedly sexually assaulting and defaming journalist E. Jean Carroll. The justices’ decision not to take up the case leaves in place a 2024 appeals court ruling that affirmed the judgment.

item.title: Strait of Hormuz. Midterm rules. Twain Prize. Heat dome. GLP-1 drugs. Ticks. Civics quiz. Catch up now.
item.description: Catch up in minutes with these 7 stories.
